title: The Pre-Echo Ridge

features.name: forming sound
features.desc-on-touch: A vibration in the air that hasn't yet learned how to speak. It trembles on the ridge like breath held just before the word begins.

features.name: the ridge itself
features.desc-on-touch: A high stone spine running through the warm dusk, half-formed like something that hasn't yet decided whether to be mountain or voice.

features.name: pre-utterance mark
features.desc-on-touch: A scar in the rock where something almost spoke. The shape of it waits, open-mouthed, for the echo that will never quite fill it.

features.name: breath-shapes
features.desc-on-touch: Patterns in the dust where air has circled without dispersing. You're watching the exact moment before a word learns to carry itself forward.

features.name: the horizon beyond speaking
features.desc-on-touch: The ridge drops away to a view of land that exists only in the instant before the first sound names it. What you see is not yet recorded.

inscription: here at the pre-echo ridge the archive learns that sound is born from silence and that both are older than memory

file.name: PreUtterance_Ridge_Frequency.wav
file.note: Spectrogram of a moment that hasn't yet decided how to resonate. The shape of the pitch is visible but the sound is unspoken.

reverie.title: REVERIE — the ridge before the word
narr: You're both standing at the place where sounds
wait to be born. She opens her mouth—not to speak,
but to listen to the breath before the speaking.
Above you, the sky is still holding its colour,
not yet released into shadow. You don't need to echo
each other here. The ridge does it for you both.

exits.label: higher still, to where echoes are still learning to dream
toward the forest source where the first sounds were never made
down the other side toward the understory's deeper throat
